summary: Comprehensive test suite covering core GitHub Actions matrix generation features with strong integration testing. Unit tests validate axis expansion, cartesian product, exclude/include rules, validation (max-size, missing axes, non-array values), and JSON serialization. Integration tests drive realistic workflows through act with three fixture scenarios, verifying exact matrix structure, exit codes, and job success. Workflow tests confirm file structure, triggers, checkout action, permissions, and actionlint validation. Good organization with clear test names and sophisticated helper functions (New-CaseRepo, Test-MatrixContains, Get-MatrixJson). Weaknesses: limited unit test edge cases (empty arrays, null values, duplicate entries, boundary conditions), some parameters untested (Compress flag, OutputPath), no parametrized tests despite opportunities, hardcoded fixture expectations without validation that actual files exist or match, minimal negative testing in integration tests. Would catch regressions in core functionality but might miss subtle edge case interactions.
